response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js, distributed systems exposure, and a solid testing culture, but he does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The most disqualifying gap is C++ experience: the role requires 8 years, and his resume shows only a brief stint at National Instruments (~6 months, Dec 2014–Jun 2015) and a bachelor thesis project using C++. C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. Additionally, his background is centered on web, product, and data-integration engineering rather than cloud compute infrastructure, and he has no documented record of leading cloud or DevOps teams at scale.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed micro-services (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second) and some cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes)
Strong engineering culture and mentoring instincts — organized recurring Refactoring Dojos, created public workshops on TDD and web internals, gave conference talks
Staff-level tenure at multiple companies with cross-stack ownership (infra setup, CI/CD, Docker, GitLab) showing breadth beyond pure feature development
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015); the role mandates 8 years — this alone is a hard disqualifier
No evidence of technical leadership specifically over cloud compute or DevOps engineers; 'Staff Engineer' titles have not translated into documented team-level cloud architecture ownership
Cloud architecture depth is shallow — AWS CDK usage at Pinepoint and Kubernetes at Northwind Labs do not constitute the production-level cloud platform experience the role demands
